title: A Room with a Viewpoint: Using Social Norms to Motivate Environmental Conservation in Hotels
citation: Goldstein, N. J., Cialdini, R. B., & Griskevicius, V. (2008). A room with a viewpoint: Using social norms to motivate environmental conservation in hotels. Journal of Consumer Research, 35(3), 472-482.

key_findings.main_effect: Descriptive norm messages (44.5%) significantly outperform environmental messages (37.2%)
key_findings.provincial_norm_effect: Same room identity (provincial norm) achieves highest participation rate (49.3%)
key_findings.robustness: Effect demonstrated in real-world field setting with actual hotel guests

critical_elements: Clear descriptive normative information (what most people do)
Provincial norm references most immediate/local setting
Binary participation measure (reuse vs not reuse)
variations: Different reference groups (guest, room, citizen, gender)
